codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly fulfills the feature requirements. Department 49 is filled with Pivot green (#6ED47C), all other departments are white (#FFFFFF), and all departments have gray stroke (#CCCCCC) with stroke-width 1.

issues.description: Stroke styling was simplified from conditional to constant. Previously, hovered/selected departments had thicker stroke (1.5px) and department 49 had a darker green stroke. Now all strokes are constant #CCCCCC with width 1. This reduces stroke-based visual feedback on hover but the fill color still changes to #E5E7EB, maintaining some hover feedback. This change aligns with the feature requirement that states 'all departments have gray stroke (#CCCCCC) for boundaries' without any special case for department 49.

The conditional fill logic uses 'else if' for hover/selected state, which means when isHighlighted is true (dept 49), the hover/selected gray fill won't apply. This is correct behavior since dept 49 should always show Pivot green, but worth noting that hovering over dept 49 doesn't change its fill from green to gray.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows AGENTS.md conventions: 4 spaces indentation, camelCase variables, PascalCase component name, typed props interface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FranceMap.tsx uses proper typing and follows the documented patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly identified that the previous implementation had conditional stroke logic (different stroke for dept 49 vs others) which violated the feature requirement of 'all departments have gray stroke'. The simplified constant stroke approach is correct per requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff notes: 'Changed strokeColor from conditional (green for dept 49, gray for others) to always #CCCCCC'

summary: PASS - The feature correctly implements the color styling requirements. Department 49 is filled with #6ED47C (Pivot green), all other departments with #FFFFFF (white), and all have #CCCCCC stroke with width 1. The only minor observation is that stroke-based hover feedback was removed, but this aligns with the explicit requirement that all strokes be gray. The implementation passes TypeScript and lint checks.
